finding: The AFT ever-coauthored RAW rate is confounded by differential authorPub indexing coverage across trees. rate_raw = rate_coverage_conditioned * both_linkable_frac, so the raw axis mixes true collaboration with how well each tree is linked in AFT authorPub.
coverage_conditioned_order: econ 0.554 < neuro 0.683 < math 0.752 < physics 0.791 < chem 0.801  (math JUMPS above neuro)
implication: math student authorPub coverage is very low (median 0 links, both_linkable 0.39); its LOW raw rate is partly an indexing artifact, not necessarily low collaboration. Whether math is genuinely low-coauth (the de-confounding linchpin) is UNRESOLVED by AFT and must be decided by the OpenAlex early-window coauth rate from the P2c builds (paper metric; bypasses AFT authorPub).
robust: econ is lowest under BOTH metrics; math is the exception where coverage adjustment flips the order vs neuro.
authoritative_axis: The reported axis is the OpenAlex early-window coauth_early_rate per discipline on the shared pub-ID resolver, in coauthorship_axis.json. The AFT proxy here is ranking-only and coverage-confounded, kept for provenance.
